fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 69 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when the number of active queries exactly equals the limit the function incorrectly allows the query instead of denying it.

constraints.intent: When active count equals limit the branch must be taken (denied), so the guard must use >= not >.
After the else branch (query allowed), active count must be strictly less than limit, not equal.
When active equals limit, the condition len(active) >= limit must hold so the denial branch is entered.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] is True and len(active__1) >= limit)

constraints.intent: The function must never return allowed=True when active count is at or above the limit.
